reviews.review_text: The gameplay is okay. Entities have cool mechanics, but the patting is very mundane. Also, the fact that players have to agree to having all voice comms captured and reviewed by devs is the biggest reason for my thumbs down. Give players privacy and autonomy.

No option to back out of REAL TIME VOICE TRANSCRIPTION while playing a silly multiplayer backrooms game? Im good. Thats right. If you dont agree, no in game voice comms. Doesnt seem like a big deal but this game uses proximity chat which you can't replicate in any VOIP.  Not using it kills the experience in my opinion. 

Again, you have to let the devs listen to EVERYTHING. There is literally no reason for this. 

Please go kick rocks on the freeway. 

Shame I cant refund...

Game was decent but it was very short considering that it came out in 2022. Should be way more maps and content and there are still some bugs. Compared to other backrooms games, this was the worst one I played so far because of the lack of content and glitches.

This game was delightful to play with a group of friends. Even in a pre-release stage, it seems reasonably well polished. My group ran into a few bugs (and weird microphone initialization issues), but nothing was of high concern. Overall, gameplay was great. 

Currently, users must agree to having transcripts of their VoIP interactions recorded, collected, and analyzed. As far as I am aware, there was no 'opt-out' option. 

Based on the agreement's terms, this data collection goes beyond moderation, but additionally claims to "improve" the game via "natural language processing" (in other words, to train an AI language model, though I do not recall reading the term 'AI' in the agreement). 

While a game can be engaging, exciting, and generally positive, I cannot recommend that users pay to have their data harvested. I appreciate the transparency of the agreement's phrasing, but this should be considered a bare minimum in the realm of consumer rights.

I love this game it definitely gives you liminal space vibes and i think its well designed. Music, white noise, graphics, game mechanics, puzzles, difficulty, unique levels and designs i think are all perfect. I mean asthetically speaking its close to perfect. My biggest complaints are the monsters. They all have the same animation and repeating sound effects. After seeing their goofy run and hearing the same noise for 30 min it just kills the immersion. i get that its still a developing game i just ask they put more effort into the entities. only other complaint is the dying experience. Almost a 100 percent chance when you die that you will be clipping through the floor or wall and shaking uncontrollably. it just looks super janky and when you die your body dissappears which kills the immersion too. other than those two things i think the game is great and if youre a fan of liminal spaces you should try it
A lot of fun with friends, has good replayability but after a couple times with different friends without updates it gets boring. ALSO THE MOVIE WAS COOL

Cool backrooms game (most of the time). The levels/puzzles can be fun but also repetitive sometimes, though that could very much be a skill issue. But you might need to redo the same level quite a few times. Regardless, I still enjoyed it :)
The game has good mechanics, graphics, and easy-to-figure-out settings and multiplayer.

The Backrooms, the famous genre of horrifying stories about falling through the floor and being in a new terrifying world. The first level, I feel, defines everything we know about The Backrooms and the famous level, and everything that follows is an attempt to recreate the mystery of the starting points. It's a game about long and tedious levels, and the majority of the level isn't even the monsters… it's food and water. It's a lame attempt for a Backrooms game that becomes extremely repetitive, and I would recommend watching someone play the game instead.

The performance on the game is kinda poor, just on the main menu my laptop's GPU hit it's thermal throttle limit and I was worried about it shutting down with how hot it got.  The post-processing even on the second lowest setting is very unoptimized, I could watch my laptop heat up several degree just toggling that one option.

The game's design seems to be more focused on having many players, if they get got by a monster you can pick them up, but someone has to lure the monster away from them before you can pick them up.  If you have only two players that's not really an option as the monster will hover over the downed player.  It also seems to take a long time for monsters to despawn after their appearance or at least to move away from a player even if you're silent.  This can lead to a lot of downtime especially in the first area where you have these holes in the wall you can hide in.

First two areas seem to be much more dangerous than they're supposed to be "canonically".  Usually it's a safe area that's devoid of both resources and entities.  In this version there's two different entities on the first level, so you don't have much opportunity to ease into things.
Easily the best Backrooms game on the market, no competition

Me and my buddy played this together and we really like it!. Just casual gamers and we get spooked pretty easily which makes it more fun. Good graphics, fun levels, and enough difficulty that we have to work for it. I would definitely say this game achieves what its setting out to do. def recommend. <3
The graphics are nice but the overall gameplay is terrible. Nearly each level gives them the same task and gets much too repetitive and no where near as fun as Escape the Backrooms.
game looks beautiful, is very fun, and is simply a well rounded game. cant wait to see what it looks like when its more developed.
Very realistic and fun to explore.

Hate. Agony. Repetition. Confusion. Buggy. Inconsistent. These are but some of the words I would use to describe my friend's and I's experience with this "game". While graphically passable, that is the only redeeming factor I can give to this dumpster fire of an experience. First level is just an unoriginal copy/paste of every other 'The Backrooms' games. Repeat this sentiment for the parking garage, the fuse juggling act, to even the boring as hell SLOG through the poolrooms. But all of that was just merely a prelude to the real show. The party rooms. Oh. My. God. The party rooms. Like 'Daisy Bell'? Well be ready to hear it play nonstop for the next several hours bozo! Even after the absolute MESS that was the party rooms you'd think the worst was over? Hahahahaha. No. The grocery store. Pure RNG hell. Let's not explain anything, punish any sort of trial and error, and on top of all that make it the most miserable hunt for nothing— running around like an absolute buffoon with empty shelf after empty shelf. Finally make it back? Nope! Killed. Try again. DO IT AGAIN. 

This is an abomination of a game. I am ashamed to say I gave a single cent to these developers. If you want a "good" Backrooms game, do yourself a favor and go buy 'Escape the Backrooms." You'll have a more enjoyable experience than whatever this sad excuse is.
Mic support is broken, seems to be a common problem according to the discord.
Game gets stuck on an infinite loading screen after completing level 0. Exiting and re-entering the game doesn't fix the issue.  Game clearly still has a lot of technical issues.

My favorite backrooms game that I have played thus far. Graphics and monster interpretations are awesome, objectives are clear, and overall this game feels really polished. It's enjoyable both alone, and with friends. There's also some fun little secrets when it comes to progressing. I've seen some people say this game is hard, however I wouldn't say its absolutely game breaking whatsoever. Some sections can definitely feel like a skill check, but I appreciate that over it being a complete walking sim. If you fail a section, it's not hard to figure out why and what you can do to succeed next run. I closely keep track of this game, as the roadmap is super promising. And every big update I've kept up with thus far has been worth replaying. This game is phenomenal

This game is the backrooms and it is good.
I left this review because I accidentally clicked bad on the opinion machine thinking it was just a part of the game. I must make clear : I enjoy the game and the graphics are muy bien

Kinda cool but theres not much too it. Like you go into a few levels and its a little tough but once you figure out the gimmick it kinda just becomes tedious. Also once you beat a level thats it. Nothing to replay or do after. Beat it in 6 ish hours and have no desire to play again.

this game is extremely funny with friends. scary while playing with friends and even more so solo. the graphics are sooo beautiful even tho i can only run low graphics without it being laggy. if you have a good pc, this is probably the most realistic backrooms game. Highly reccomend, amazing price.

really fun with friends there are some bugs but its only really the shopping cart in one of the last levels of the game and even though I completed the fun level I didn't get the achievement and I don't realty want to replay it just to get it but besides that its really good there are 11 levels and more to come I had two other friends playing but I'm sure it would be more fun to play with more knowing that up to 6 people can play and the movie event is just a backrooms movie tape and a room and you get to see a clip from the movie.

I have spent countless hours playing this game and never have not enjoyed every moment of it, i do very much reccomend this to others aslong as you have friends to play with,  this game is the absolute best game to play if ur looking for a bit of adrenaline and a bit of laughter. 100% RECCOMEND!

Be warned there are bugs multiplayer crashes disconnects and loading issues. when you can get it to work its a really cool game though. I would advise trying out your connection within the refund time window

Its definitely a blast with your friends, and if you want a challenge then do the "Purgatory Difficulty" and if it makes a difference I mainly use controller and this game easily flows with KB&M or Controller.

very realistic and fun aff

É um jogo bem mais ou menos, bem mais pro menos. Tem uns bagulhos mó nada a ver, por exemplo, no level 232 os inimigos ficam em modo de perseguição por razão nenhuma. O level 6 é muito mal feito, principalmente por esse jogo ser muito mal otimizado.
Acredito que isso pode ser melhorado, mas para um game de 2022, tendo tão poucos levels e uma IA tão broxante e injusta na maior parte do tempo, eu sinto que esse jogo tem muito potencial, se os devs ouvirem mais sua comunidade e, claro, otimizarem melhor o jogo.
Its really scary and fun and the graphics are amazing. The puzzles are difficult unless you look it up which me and my friend had to do.
This game is dope as ♥♥♥♥ and fun as hell. I really love the aesthetic and graphics and I'm really looking forward to future levels and updates.

Loved the proximity chat, and the accurate variety of levels. Also the graphics in this game was incredible. Overall, super fun. There is a solo option but nothing beats playing with friends and I look forward to new levels and updates.
so cool game and graphics is exceptional

I'm not even 10 hours into the gameplay, the mechanics of the game as well as the diversity in the levels is different than anything I've come across. Honestly this game gives me the same vibe as when fnaf was released!

Backrooms Escape Together is a fun game for Backrooms fans

The graphics are great, procedural level generation keeps things fresh, the entities and level design are challenging, but fair. I like the whistle mechanic to help find teammates. I wish the camcorder was used in more than just one level, it really adds to the atmosphere of the game.

Problems so far are bugged achievements and sometimes loading save files get stuck in a infinite loop. I also think the game could use some more challenging puzzles.

The roadmap looks good, but is ambitious and will take years to complete the game.

Now which Backrooms game is better? I think Escape the Backrooms will give a player a complete Backrooms experience and is the better game right now. But Backrooms Escape Together has a more authentic feel to the lore and is slowing getting better. As a Backrooms fan, you need both games :)
This is by far the best backrooms game, innovative, replayable, challenging, fun. Literally everything you would want out of a backrooms game
Fun, eerie, really cool concept. You feel the tension, being afraid, like you have to watch your back. Great balance between difficulty and playfulness.
played with 2 other friends one of the best experiences i have ever had it was scary cant wait for more levels
From graphics to fear factor and suspense to audio this ticked all the boxes holy what a game always looking for when there is a new update for this

This is probably the best multiplayer experience for the backrooms setting. My friends and I have played many backrooms games over the past few years both single player and multiplayer, and many of those in the past week in preparation for the A24 backrooms film. This one is the best for multiplayer certainly, I definitely would recommend playing for a fun time with buddies. Also probably some of the best graphics and level design out of the multiplayer backrooms games as well.

не советую проходить в соло мультиплеере, левел ! (беги за свою жизнь или же run for your life) наитупейшее говно просто. пока ты бегаешь от рыгающего чувырлы, тебе надо найти ключ карту которая может быть в тупике из которого ты живым уже не уйдешь, так еще такой процесс надо повторить ДВА РАЗА. если уж играть то по возможности до этого уровня, жопа у меня сгорела полностью. не советую

Me and my friend were on what scientists are calling “the cleanest Backrooms run ever recorded.”

Entities dodged. Puzzles solved. Morale high. World record pace. History was being made.

Then I encountered the most dangerous monster in the game:
a gap between two sets of stairs.

Not a cliff. Not a hole. Not a pit into the void. A drop so small a Roomba could survive it with mild emotional damage.

I stepped down, instantly died, and watched our world record run get executed by basic architecture.

10/10 horror game.
The monsters are scary, but OSHA violations are scarier.

Hello,

I would like to report an issue regarding the Arabic language support in Backrooms Escape Together.

When I first purchased and played the game, Arabic was available and worked quite well throughout the entire game. However, after one of the recent updates (the update that added new levels), the Arabic text in some of the new content became corrupted and displayed as question marks or unreadable symbols instead of proper Arabic text.

Today, after installing the latest update, I noticed that the Arabic language option appears to have been removed completely from the game, even though the Steam store page still lists Arabic as a supported language.

Could you please look into this issue and consider restoring the Arabic language support? Arabic-speaking players would greatly appreciate it. The game was enjoyable to play in Arabic, and it would be unfortunate to lose that accessibility.

Thank you for your time and for your continued work on the game.

Best regards.

bruh this game is to fun with friends and i swear its hard and challenging i say tell your friend to buy the game and you buy it play togther for the fun adventure and or buy it to your family or friend w game w owner w devolper w all bye everyone

Favorite backrooms game so far. My playtime is 2 playthroughs. Levels are really well done and have some unique takes on them, the detail in the camera mode for pictures is excellent, and it's really enjoyable with friends. Minimal bugs, though the most persistent ones are audio issues. These include: Not being able to hear friends when you are spectating and; microphone not functioning properly in game in the queue, during spectating, and when loading into a new level. This is not always the case, but it is present enough to note. I also wish some of the puzzles were more complex. Otherwise, no complaints! Can't wait for more levels! My friends and I will be hopping back in for them.
This game is a thrilling and energetic adventure! There were many funny moments with the entities and friends. I recommend it!
Best horror game I've played with my friends as it provides some good puzzles and graphics, not too scary though

Probably the best backrooms game. Smooth gameplay and imersive levels without too many lazy gimmicks.

This game holds a perfect meaning of the backrooms, while also allowing you to experience it with your FRIENDS. Giving you an eerie, funny feeling, without removing the main concept of this game which is horror.
OVERALL: Absolutely recommended

I am really enjoying this game. I feel I would add some customization, it gets confusing to play with friends. It's a fun game so far. Also one of my friends was having problems with the game and he has a decent computer.

Constant infinite looping loading screens when joining lobbies. I attempted to host one but get kicked back to the main menu :( Can't really recommend a game that doesn't seem to work. I have tried all the recommended fixes online but no luck and honestly having to resort to such fixes shouldn't be a thing. Please fix these issues.

I'm not impressed.
Let me begin by saying I had a great time playing this game - but that was only because of the group I was playing with. ( Friends can make anything fun, take a look at roblox )

The quality of this game undershot my expectations by quite a bit. The novelty of new levels wears off pretty quickly, the game isn't scary much at all, and this feels very bare bones for an unreal engine game.

One pro I will mention is the mechanic of the pitch black scanner level is a very interesting and fun mechanic to play with. Unfortunately, you get one level of it in the main game progression. The rest of the game feels like something that was made for a game jam. With the exception of the scanner level, you could pretty much rebuild the entire game in a month with nothing but youtube tutorials. It just feels a little low effort and low quality for my taste. And keeping in mind that this game released almost 4 years ago, 6 hours of playtime isn't great.

If you want a fun night or two with your friends, and you are willing to spend 10 bucks for around 10 hours of playtime, go ahead - but for me personally, I would like a little more polish and some replay-ability. Make the game scarier, make the cosmetics collectible, add some mechanics, smooth out the rough edges. 

TL:DR, I had fun, friends carried the game ( in terms of enjoyment ), want to see more out of the game itself. 5/10. If I could give neutral I would.

I will be returning to re-evaluate this review once the game gets out of early access.

[h1]The definitive Backrooms multiplayer experience[/h1]
As someone who didn't even really know about the Backrooms until just a few months ago, and having spent enough time looking at the most popular interpretations and adaptations of the concept, I think this game is by far one of the best, if not the best version you can play with your friends right now.

What it clearly lacks being an Early Access product, it makes up in strides for its stunning graphical fidelity, it's care to immerse you with found footage camera POVs and level design, and it's simple but effective survival horror mechanics that all come together quite well for a game that's not even finished yet. The potential is quite honestly sky-high.

Yes, the AI can sometimes be quite jank with audio that could be further fleshed out.
Yes, the single player experience could do with significant work and balancing.
Yes, it's not the largest content amount on offer compared to [i]other[/i] Backrooms games.

None of that was a dealbreaker for me, as this was clear quality over quantity that is still getting updated (if a bit slower than we'd like), but my friends and I think it's well worth the wait if the devs keep at it.

[b]And considering A24 actually collaborated with this game for the film, I can't think of any better evidence to suggest you consider this one of the best avenues to explore the Backrooms out there[/b]
I have never been more scared of a backrooms horror great game great graphics doesn't overly rely on screamers ran great

Some of the puzzles and levels can either be a bit too long (still lookin at you even after the changes, Station) or short, but overall the game does an amazing job of making everything feel as surreal as its supposed to. Definitely a recommend to anyone wanting to experience the Backrooms.
